overview.system_goal: Simulate multi-channel information diffusion in social networks through social relationships and recommendation algorithms, analyzing the impact of different recommendation strategies on network evolution.
overview.environment_description: Agents are connected by social relationships, and each agent can receive content from different kinds of recommendation systems and social networks.
overview.workflow_structure: From the start, through sequential agent interactions, and ends when the condition is met.

design_concepts.interaction_patterns: Recommendation algorithms and social networks recommend content to users in parallel. Users make decisions based on recommendations and can further spread information through mentions.
design_concepts.communication_protocols: RecommenderAgent recommends content to all UserAgents in each round. If a UserAgent publishes new content, it recommends to all fans. Information can be further spread through mentions.
design_concepts.decision_mechanisms: Decision factors include the overlap between notes and interests, social relationships, and activity levels.
design_concepts.execution_flow: Start → RecommenderAgent && UserAgent → UserAgent [→ UserAgent]...[→ UserAgent] → End

details.agent_behaviors: Recommendation algorithms recommend content to all UserAgents. If a UserAgent publishes new content, it recommends to all fans. UserAgents receiving messages must decide in the current round whether to comment or mention others. All mentions are also processed in the current round.
details.decision_algorithms: Algorithms consider the overlap between notes and interests, social relationships, and activity levels to determine what actions agents should take.
details.specific_constraints: Initialization involves creating a specified number of agents and loading their profile information. The environment also needs to be updated, retrieving previous_notes and current_notes from content_pool for specified time periods, and updating current_timestep.
details.process_sequence: 1) Start initialization, 2) Recommendation algorithms recommend content to all UserAgents, and UserAgents with new content recommend to all fans, 3) UserAgents receiving messages must decide in the current round whether to comment or mention others, 4) If receiving a mention, choose whether to reply, 5) After all agents act, check termination conditions, 6) Either terminate or start the next round.

agent_types.UserAgent: Represents homogeneous users in social networks, with attributes such as id, nickname, gender, description, ip_location, follow_count, fan_count, interaction, user interest tag list(interest_tags), activity level and etc.
agent_types.RecommenderAgent: Represents recommendation systems. Includes hot topic recommendation, interest-based recommendation, and random recommendation.

metrics.name: Comment Generation Over Time
metrics.description: Tracks the number of comments generated over time to measure information diffusion activity. The x-axis represents time steps, and the y-axis represents the total number of comments at the current time step.
metrics.calculation_logic: Retrieve content_pool from environment data and count the total number of comments in all notes' comments dictionaries. Each call returns the total number of comments at the current time step. The monitoring system automatically records the time series and plots a line chart.

metrics.name: Comment Volume Real Time
metrics.description: 按评论 timestamp（真实时间）统计：上图累计评论量（折线+标记），下图各时间桶内条数（柱状图，柱宽等于桶宽、左右相接）；横轴为真实时间，非仿真步。
metrics.calculation_logic: Collect all comment timestamps from content_pool, sort, histogram with adaptive bin count; monitor: cumulative line+markers + touching bar histogram per raw_data._viz_kind comment_realtime.

metrics.name: Comment Top-Level vs Reply Over Time
metrics.description: 每个采样步：统计 content_pool 中无父评论（顶层）与有父评论（回复）的条数随时间变化；导出为上下两张折线图。
metrics.calculation_logic: Count comments with empty parent_comment_id vs non-empty; returns two scalars per tick for dual line subplots.

metrics.name: Comment Count Frequency
metrics.description: Two line charts (markers): (1) X = total comments per user (0,1,2,…), denominator = users appearing as note author or commenter in content_pool; Y = % with exactly k comments. (2) X = comments under each note (0,1,2,…), Y = % of all notes. Exports *_commenting_users.png and *_notes.png.
metrics.calculation_logic: Distribution of per-user total comment counts (denominator: note authors ∪ commenters in pool; k=0 included) and per-note comment counts (denominator: all notes); values as percentages; monitor plots as line+marker PNGs; raw_data._viz_kind comment_count_freq_bar; n_users_in_pool.

metrics.name: Posting User Comment Behavior
metrics.description: 每个采样时刻：列出所有发过帖的用户，统计其本帖一跳/本帖回复/他帖评论条数；导出 metrics 图时在同目录写入表格 CSV；并追加写入 metrics 目录下 posting_user_comment_behavior/snapshots.jsonl（含 simulation_step）。
metrics.calculation_logic: Per posting user: own-note top-level, own-note replies, on others' notes; plot export writes users table as CSV; JSONL snapshots per monitor tick.

metrics.name: Comment Similarity
metrics.description: BGE 向量相似度均值：生成评论与参考评论按 (note_id, user_id) 对齐后，计算 mean(cosine_similarity)。需环境提供 reference_json_path 或 reference_comments。
metrics.calculation_logic: 从 content_pool 提取生成评论，从 reference_json_path 或 reference_comments 读取参考评论，按 (note_id, user_id) 配对后调用 BGE embedding 算均值。若无参考或无配对则返回 None。
